purpose: Implementation-grade invention specs for making Kolm materially better across quantization, compilation, distillation, runtime, privacy, cloud, proof, registry, agents, device, compliance, and developer experience surfaces.

research_sources.use: rotation-based outlier reduction for W/A/KV quantization
incoherence and lattice codebooks for extreme compression
additive quantization for extreme model compression
KV paging and serving throughput model
LLM judge and arena-style evaluation caveats
artifact signing and transparency log pattern
provider-specific SFT availability and deprecation risk
Claude cache semantics, isolation, TTL, and cache diagnostics

inventions.name: Kolm-Q Max Mixed-Precision Quantization Compiler
inventions.thesis: The best quantizer for Kolm is a constrained planner that chooses per-layer weight, activation, and KV-cache precision from task loss sensitivity, activation outliers, runtime kernels, and proof gates.
math_core: For each layer l and candidate method m, estimate loss_l,m = trace(H_l * deltaW_l,m * deltaW_l,m^T) + alpha * activation_outlier_l,m + beta * kv_error_l,m.
Solve min sum_l loss_l,m + lambda_s * size_m + lambda_t * latency_m subject to K-score >= gate, memory <= device_budget, runtime_kernel_supported=true, and privacy_policy_allowed=true.
Use Pareto pruning over quality, size, latency, and proof cost before running expensive holdout evals.
build_phases: Add calibration slice builder from train rows, holdout rows, synthetic stress rows, and redacted hot prompts.
Collect Hessian-proxy, activation percentile, outlier rotation, and KV-pressure statistics per layer.
Generate GPTQ/AWQ/SmoothQuant/rotation/KV candidates and mark runtime kernel support from compute registry.
Run constrained solver, then short holdout and adversarial eval for top candidates.
Emit quantization receipt with calibration hashes, layer bit map, skipped layers, measured smoke, K-score delta, and rollback target.

expected: at least 3 math equations and 5 build phases are present

acceptance_tests: Planner is deterministic for fixed calibration hashes.
Planner refuses to use holdout-only rows for training or calibration leakage.
Receipt verifier fails if any layer bit map, calibration hash, or measured K-score delta changes.
CLI prints a Pareto table and a single recommended plan with explicit tradeoffs.
failure_modes: Runtime kernel unsupported for selected bit layout.
Short-holdout K-score passes but adversarial eval fails.
Quantized artifact is smaller but slower because target runtime lacks fused kernels.
Calibration slice includes privacy-denied rows.

inventions.name: KolmIR Proof-Preserving AutoTarget Compiler
inventions.thesis: Kolm should compile evidence, policy, runtime, and receipts into a product-level IR that lowers into GGUF, ONNX, CoreML, MLX, ExecuTorch, TensorRT-LLM, vLLM, SGLang, WASM, and edge bundles without losing proof parity.
math_core: Represent each compile as DAG G=(V,E) with nodes for data, eval, model, transform, runtime, policy, receipt, and deployment.
A lowering is valid only if for every proof node p in G there exists a target proof p' where hash(p') = hash(normalize(p, target)).
Choose target by maximizing utility = quality_gate_pass - lambda_latency*p95 - lambda_cost*unit_cost + lambda_portability*target_coverage + lambda_enterprise*policy_fit.
build_phases: Define KolmIR schema and canonical JSON encoding.
Add passes for split proof, distill proof, quant proof, runtime policy, and deployment target.
Add target lowerers for GGUF, ONNX, CoreML, MLX, ExecuTorch, TensorRT-LLM, vLLM/SGLang, WASM, and edge.
Add verifier that checks proof parity between source IR and target manifest.
Add CLI preview that shows what is target-specific and what is target-invariant.

expected: all target lowerers have named proof-preservation requirements
open runtime-wasm and ecosystem-adoption gates are covered

acceptance_tests: Same source IR lowered to two targets preserves dataset, eval, K-score, and receipt hashes.
Target unsupported features fail with actionable diagnostics.
OpenAPI exposes the compile plan and target preview without leaking secrets.
CLI `kolm compile --target auto --explain` prints target choice rationale.
failure_modes: Target lowerer drops a sidecar receipt.
Target runtime changes tokenization and invalidates K-score comparability.
Compiler optimizes latency by silently changing policy semantics.

inventions.name: Distill Forge Max Teacher-Student Optimizer
inventions.thesis: The best distillation product optimizes expected K-score lift per dollar by choosing collect, no-train, SFT, KD, rationale, preference, rejection, and on-policy loops from evidence rather than blindly launching fine-tunes.
math_core: For strategy s, estimate EV(s)=P(pass_gate|s)*value_quality - cost_teacher(s) - cost_compute(s) - risk_privacy(s) - latency_to_artifact(s).
Select examples by marginal utility u_i = expected_error_reduction_i / (teacher_tokens_i + label_cost_i + privacy_penalty_i).
Use off-policy replay first; promote to on-policy only when distribution shift score exceeds threshold.
build_phases: Add strategy scorer for no-train, rules, cache, RAG, SFT, KD, rationale SFT, preference, rejection, and on-policy.
Add teacher provider planner across OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter, local, and managed train backends.
Add active example queue that prioritizes highest K-score uncertainty and highest business frequency.
Add privacy membrane check before any teacher call or training export.
Add compile diagnostics that explain why not enough examples, noisy labels, ambiguous task, or bad holdout stopped the run.

expected: cost and quality tradeoff is explicit

acceptance_tests: Planner recommends no-train when cache/rules/RAG beats fine-tune EV.
Planner recommends Anthropic and OpenAI paths when both keys/configs are available.
Planner refuses synthetic-only promotion without real holdout evidence.
Output includes example acquisition plan and expected K-score lift range.
failure_modes: Teacher changes model behavior without version pin.
Preference data is sparse and overfits subjective labels.
Rationale supervision leaks hidden chain-of-thought policy or private data.
On-policy loop drifts from tenant distribution.

inventions.name: Active K-Lift Dataset Curriculum
inventions.thesis: Kolm should turn captured calls into a ranked curriculum where each candidate row has expected K-score lift, privacy risk, label cost, and coverage value before it can enter train or holdout splits.
math_core: For row x, score = uncertainty(x)*frequency(x)*coverage_gap(x) - privacy_risk(x) - label_cost(x) - duplicate_penalty(x).
Holdout rows maximize coverage and adversarial diversity while minimizing semantic overlap with train rows.
Curriculum batches are accepted only if train/holdout minhash distance and row-hash disjointness pass.
build_phases: Add row featurizer over traces, errors, latency outliers, redaction flags, and task clusters.
Add active label queue with deterministic ranking and tenant RBAC.
Add train/holdout splitter with semantic overlap warning and hash lock.
Add dataset receipt that records rejected rows and reasons.
Add account/CLI API response shape for next best rows to label.

expected: holdout independence and privacy metrics covered

acceptance_tests: Duplicate or near-duplicate train/holdout rows are rejected.
Rows with privacy-denied flags cannot be selected for teacher calls.
Queue ranking is deterministic for fixed lake snapshot.
Dataset receipt explains selected and rejected rows.
failure_modes: High-frequency rows dominate and reduce tail coverage.
Semantic dedupe misses paraphrases.
Label queue leaks private prompts into UI without redaction.

inventions.name: SpecCache Runtime Planner
inventions.thesis: Kolm should choose exact cache, semantic cache, prompt compression, speculative decoding, fallback routing, and local artifact execution per request from latency/cost/quality constraints.
math_core: Expected request utility = cache_hit_prob*cache_value + spec_accept_rate*decode_savings + fallback_success_prob*availability - quality_risk - policy_risk.
Use prefix tree and semantic embedding neighborhoods as separate caches with different proof receipts.
Speculative decoding is enabled only if accepted-token estimate exceeds verification overhead under current runtime.
build_phases: Add runtime decision engine that scores exact cache, semantic cache, artifact local, provider call, fallback, and speculative modes.
Add per-request receipt with chosen path, cache hit/miss reason, fallback reason, and OTEL span id.
Add acceptance-rate estimator for Medusa/EAGLE/draft-style local runtime heads where supported.
Add streaming normalizer integration so speculative/fallback paths preserve SSE contract.
Add route and CLI explain output.
acceptance_tests: Cache hit receipt never claims model inference occurred.
Fallback chain preserves tenant policy and token budget.
Streaming path emits normalized events across provider and local artifact modes.
Explain output includes why a cheaper route was rejected.
failure_modes: Semantic cache returns stale or policy-incompatible answer.
Speculative path changes output distribution when lossless mode is required.
Fallback provider violates data residency policy.

inventions.name: Adapter Fabric Multi-Tenant LoRA Runtime
inventions.thesis: Kolm should serve many tenant adapters and artifact deltas against shared base models with admission control, proof receipts, and private registry ownership instead of spawning one runtime per artifact.
math_core: Batch admission maximizes sum tenant_value_i subject to GPU_memory(base + active_adapters + KV) <= budget and fairness_i >= min_share.
Adapter co-location score = shared_base_match + runtime_kernel_support - policy_conflict - noisy_neighbor_risk.
Each inference receipt binds base hash, adapter hash, registry version, and runtime batch id.
build_phases: Add adapter registry metadata and dependency graph from artifact to base model.
Add runtime scheduler for shared base + many adapter deltas.
Add fairness and quota checks per tenant/artifact.
Add receipts binding base, adapter, batch, and output hash.
Add cost attribution by base amortization and adapter usage.
acceptance_tests: Two tenants sharing a base model cannot see each other's adapter metadata unless policy allows.
Scheduler refuses adapters with incompatible tokenizer/base hash.
Cost report shows base amortization and adapter-specific usage.
Receipt verifier detects swapped adapter hash.
failure_modes: Noisy neighbor adapter consumes KV/cache and hurts p95 latency.
Adapter/base mismatch produces silent quality drop.
Private registry policy bypass leaks adapter listing.

inventions.name: Adaptive Eval Fuzzer
inventions.thesis: K-score should become a calibrated, adversarial, task-typed statistical contract that searches for failures instead of only scoring a static holdout.
math_core: Find adversarial cases by maximizing expected_failure_prob(x) * production_frequency(x) * severity(x) - eval_cost(x).
Report K-score as task_metric with confidence interval and judge calibration, not a single unqualified scalar.
Promotion requires lower confidence bound >= gate for critical axes.
build_phases: Add task-typed eval registry with extraction, classification, generation, privacy, code, legal, translation, and safety axes.
Add fuzzers for paraphrase, boundary, injection, formatting, language, and policy adversaries.
Add judge calibration curves and false-accept counter.
Add public benchmark evidence export schema for raw hashes and non-secret outputs.
Add CI gate that refuses marketing claim readiness without public benchmark reports.

smoke_simulation.command: npm run verify:quality-calibration && npm run verify:benchmark-evidence
expected: judge fixture has zero false accepts
public benchmark claims remain gated without raw report lanes

acceptance_tests: Failing adversarial case blocks production-ready promotion.
Benchmark evidence validator rejects raw prompts, outputs, and secret values.
K-score API returns confidence and calibration context.
Marketing claim readiness stays false until public evidence exists.
failure_modes: Judge model is overconfident on adversarial prompt variants.
Static holdout becomes contaminated by training data.
Benchmark report omits pricing or hardware context.

inventions.name: Privacy Membrane Max Policy Compiler
inventions.thesis: Privacy should be compiled as a measurable policy graph across capture, redaction, zero retention, differential privacy, teacher calls, training exports, and receipts.
math_core: Policy decision d = allow iff all controls c in path(input -> capture -> teacher -> train -> artifact) satisfy c.scope >= data_class and c.receipt_hash is present.
Redaction confidence combines deterministic detector, ML detector, context class, and fail-closed threshold.
DP budget composes epsilon across aggregate queries and refuses export when tenant budget is exceeded.
build_phases: Define policy graph schema for data class, store mode, teacher mode, training mode, and export mode.
Attach policy receipt to capture, dataset, distill, compile, runtime, and compliance export events.
Add redaction benchmark per PII/PHI class and fail-closed unknown detector states.
Add DP budget ledger by tenant/namespace.
Add account and CLI explain response for why data can or cannot move.

smoke_simulation.command: npm run verify:redaction-benchmark && npm run verify:compliance-packet

acceptance_tests: Zero-retention call leaves no capture/event row.
Teacher export is blocked for privacy-denied data class.
DP ledger composes and refuses over-budget aggregate export.
Policy receipt verifier detects tampered data movement path.
failure_modes: Regex redactor misses context-dependent PHI.
ML detector unavailable and system fails open.
DP budget not tenant-scoped.
Account UI shows raw prompt in privacy error.

inventions.name: Constraint-First Cloud Compute Broker
inventions.thesis: Users should specify constraints and proof boundary; Kolm should quote, launch, monitor, import, and verify train/distill/quant jobs across local, SSH, rented GPUs, managed train, BYOC, and object storage.
math_core: Select backend b that minimizes expected_total_cost = gpu_cost + storage_cost + egress_cost + failure_retry_cost + privacy_penalty while satisfying deadline, residency, and toolchain constraints.
Use two-phase job state: quote -> reserved -> running -> imported -> verified; only verified jobs can produce artifact claims.
Artifact import accepts only if output hashes, logs, eval receipt, and storage receipt match the job plan.
build_phases: Unify local, SSH, RunPod, Modal, AWS, Together, and BYOC providers behind broker interface.
Add quote model and dry-run mode that never launches spend.
Add object-storage import/export receipts for R2/S3/Supabase/local.
Add webhook/event state machine for job status and failure recovery.
Add cost attribution and policy receipts per job.

expected: command is spend-safe until user executes

acceptance_tests: No provider API call occurs in dry-run simulation.
Missing storage credentials produce setup hints, not stack traces.
Job import fails if output hash differs from job receipt.
BYOC job keeps secrets out of artifact manifest.
failure_modes: Provider changes GPU SKU availability after quote.
Object storage lacks required checksum semantics.
Webhook arrives out of order.
Rented GPU region violates residency policy.

inventions.name: Device Fit Autopilot
inventions.thesis: Kolm should score every artifact/model/export against actual memory, runtime kernels, latency target, battery envelope, privacy mode, and fallback before telling a user it can run on a device.
math_core: Fit score = w_mem*memory_margin + w_kernel*kernel_support + w_latency*latency_margin + w_quality*K_delta + w_policy*privacy_fit - fallback_risk.
Recommend export target only if lower confidence bound of device latency estimate <= target p95.
Fallback plan must preserve privacy mode and proof mode.
build_phases: Add device probe schema for CPU/GPU/NPU/RAM/browser/OS/runtime versions.
Add model/artifact estimator for memory, prefill, decode, KV cache, and file size.
Add target recommendation API and CLI.
Add runtime telemetry opt-in importer to refine estimates.
Add mobile/browser/edge package docs from package readiness.

expected: runtime-wasm and ios-android-sdk package gates covered

acceptance_tests: Low-memory device gets smaller artifact or cloud fallback recommendation.
Unsupported NPU runtime is never advertised as available.
Telemetry import is opt-in and strips prompt/output content.
Account and CLI show the same fit result.
failure_modes: Browser WebGPU availability differs by user setting.
Device thermal throttling invalidates p95 estimate.
Fallback route violates offline-only requirement.

inventions.name: Signed Agent Tool Compiler
inventions.thesis: Kolm should compile repeated agent behavior into signed MCP and local tools with permission receipts, dependency graphs, and replay evals instead of only exposing an MCP server.
math_core: Compile candidate if repeated_tool_trace_frequency * avoided_tokens * success_rate exceeds maintenance_cost + policy_risk.
Tool permission set is minimized by solving set cover over observed successful actions and required resources.
Replay eval must pass before MCP config can be emitted.
build_phases: Mine repeated agent traces from capture lake with privacy policy filter.
Infer tool schema, permission manifest, and deterministic replay cases.
Compile MCP server/tool sidecar and signed .kolm artifact.
Add permission receipt and dependency graph.
Add installer for Claude Desktop, Cursor, Cline, Continue, and local MCP.
acceptance_tests: Compiled tool cannot request permissions absent from permission receipt.
Replay eval fails on changed output schema.
MCP installer writes project-local config only unless user approves global install.
Dependency graph shows every external tool/resource.
failure_modes: Trace mining captures one-off behavior that should not be compiled.
Tool permission inference is over-broad.
Agent sidecar changes behavior without a new receipt.

inventions.name: ReceiptOS Unified Evidence Fabric
inventions.thesis: Every product action should emit a linked, signed, privacy-safe receipt so buyers can verify dataset, eval, compile, quant, runtime, fallback, billing, and compliance claims end to end.
math_core: Receipt chain is valid if every edge e=(a,b) has hash(b.input_refs) containing hash(a.output) and every signature verifies under active key epoch.
Public receipt projection removes payload fields but preserves hashes, timestamps, policy decisions, and verifier status.
Audit completeness = observed_required_receipts / expected_required_receipts per journey.
build_phases: Define receipt graph schema and key epoch policy.
Emit receipts from capture, split, label, distill, compile, quant, runtime, fallback, deploy, billing, and compliance export.
Add public/private projection modes.
Add verifier that traverses receipt graph and explains missing links.
Add SIEM/OTEL export with receipt ids.
acceptance_tests: Verifier fails when any receipt edge is missing.
Public projection contains no prompt/output/secrets.
Key rotation preserves old receipt verification.
Compliance export links to receipt ids and not raw data.
failure_modes: Receipt graph is too verbose and slows runtime path.
Projection accidentally leaks sensitive metadata.
Key epoch metadata is missing for old artifacts.

inventions.name: Verified Artifact Market Maker
inventions.thesis: The registry should rank artifacts by verified utility per task, runtime, license, device, cost, risk, and evidence depth, not by downloads or stale scalar K-score.
math_core: Rank score = task_fit*K_confidence + runtime_fit + license_fit + evidence_depth + publisher_trust - dependency_risk - freshness_penalty.
Version diff risk = semantic_change + data_change + model_change + policy_change + dependency_blast_radius.
Enterprise installability requires license, SBOM, publisher, provenance, and private-registry policy all pass.
build_phases: Add artifact scorecard from manifest, receipts, evals, runtime support, license, publisher, and dependencies.
Add version diff with policy/data/model/proof sections.
Add private registry policy and dependency blast-radius filters.
Add deploy button compatibility checks.
Add marketplace revenue/share metadata without allowing unverified paid artifacts.

expected: registry readiness and conversion metric covered

acceptance_tests: Unverified artifact cannot outrank verified artifact for regulated filter.
Diff viewer shows model/data/policy changes separately.
Private registry denies cross-tenant artifact pull.
Deploy button disabled when target runtime unsupported.
failure_modes: Ranking becomes pay-to-win.
License metadata is missing or wrong.
Dependency graph misses nested artifact sidecar.

inventions.name: CodeGraph Product Autopilot
inventions.thesis: A local product code graph should bind source symbols, routes, docs, CLI verbs, tests, readiness gates, and product promises so agents can implement without rediscovering the codebase.
math_core: Graph nodes are files, symbols, routes, docs, tests, readiness ids, CLI verbs, and UI links; edges are imports, handlers, coverage, claims, and generated outputs.
Implementation route = shortest verified path from requirement to owning files and tests, penalizing stale generated artifacts.
Agent risk score increases when claimed readiness lacks a route/test/doc edge.
build_phases: Index repo into product graph with symbols and route ownership.
Attach docs and tests to readiness ids.
Add stale-generated-artifact detector.
Add CLI `kolm codegraph why <route|file|requirement>`.
Emit agent task packets with exact file ownership and verification gates.

expected: developer-experience metric lift is highest for this category

acceptance_tests: Every non-stub route maps to product surface and OpenAPI operation.
Every open readiness gate maps to closeout evidence and owner files.
CLI explains why generated artifact is stale.
Agent packet includes disjoint edit scope and tests.
failure_modes: Generated graph drifts from router.
Graph misses dynamic imports or generated docs.
Agent follows stale owner file path.
